name: Banded Lateral Walk
resistance_profile: Loop-band tension rises as the hip abducts, so the glute medius works hardest in the shortened, abducted position — a contraction-biased isolation.
cues: Stay low in the quarter-squat for the whole set
Keep constant tension — don't let the band go slack
Toes forward, drive the knee out rather than up
execution_steps: Place a loop band around both legs, just above the knees, or around the ankles to make it harder.
Drop into an athletic quarter-squat with feet hip-width, chest up and tension already on the band.
Step one foot out to the side against the band, keeping the toes pointed forward.
Bring the trailing foot in only part-way so band tension stays constant, then continue stepping.
Walk a set number of steps one direction, then reverse to work the other side.
good_when: You want to target the glute medius and hip stability
You need a warm-up or activation drill before squats and hinges
You only have a band (home or travel)
bad_when: You want a primary driver of glute-max mass
You need heavy, precisely progressable load
common_errors: Standing up tall between steps and losing band tension
Rushing or bouncing the steps instead of controlling each one
progressions: Use a thicker band or move it down to the ankles
Increase step distance and squat depth
regressions: Use a lighter band or place it above the knees
